content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Thorough review of the planned-elsewhere reconciliation feature. The implementation is well-designed with careful attention to durability (journal-first writes, reconstruction from aggregates), idempotency (dedup by identity key, durable-before-comment pattern to prevent duplicate br comments on replay), backward compatibility (ProgressUpdated with sub_type discriminator, #[serde(other)] Unknown variant), and safety (conservative merge clearing mapped_to_bead_id on reviewer disagreement, fail-closed validation against the allowed PE bead set extracted from the prompt). The multi-layer round supersession logic (journal sentinels + aggregate-based fallback) correctly handles edge cases where sentinel writes fail. All 38+ new tests pass, compilation is clean, and the code handles failure modes at each step (transient vs definitive errors, journal-sidecar divergence, cross-run supersession, reconstruction from aggregates when journal writes fail). No blocking bugs, safety problems, or correctness gaps found.

## Amendments

None.
